title: A ‘once-in-a-lifetime’ weekend on Boston Harbor
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Dozens of tall ships paraded into Boston Harbor for Sail Boston 2026, featuring ships like Amerigo Vespucci and ARA Libertad.
Three New England hotels ranked among the world's best by Travel + Leisure, including XV Beacon in Boston at No. 55.
A new boutique hotel, The Harborvale, opened on Lake Champlain in Burlington, Vermont, offering sunset toasts and live music.

title: 2026-27 Bruins lineup 1.0: Predicting Boston’s lines, D pairs after start to offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Fraser Minten could join David Pastrnak and Morgan Geekie on the Bruins' top line for the 2026-27 season.
Frederic Brunet is paired with Charlie McAvoy, despite McAvoy's six-game suspension at the start of the season.
Jeremy Swayman will remain the primary goalie with Michael DiPietro as backup, following DiPietro's strong AHL performance.

title: 12-acre horse farm in MetroWest lists for $1.9m
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Little Brook Farm in Carlisle, a 12.6-acre horse farm, is listed for $1,995,000.
The property includes a farmhouse, custom-designed barn, and equestrian facilities with leased horse stalls.
Possibility exists to subdivide the land, with trails accessing conservation areas like Great Brook State Park.

title: N.H. police chief placed on leave after video released of him grabbing someone by the throat inside police station
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Gorham, NH Police Chief Jimmy Willhoite placed on leave after video shows him grabbing a man by the throat.
The incident is under investigation by the NH Department of Justice Public Integrity Unit and Police Standards Council.
Stephen Gregory claims he was assaulted by Willhoite during a police station confrontation after reporting a threat.

title: Man, 29, killed in daytime shooting in Dorchester
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 29-year-old man was shot and killed on York St. in Dorchester, Boston, Wednesday morning.
Police and EMS responded to the scene, where the man was declared dead. His identity remains undisclosed.
Authorities have not made any arrests and urge anyone with information to contact the Boston Police CrimeStoppers Program.

title: U.S. Marshals arrest 2 alleged fugitives weeks after mass shooting in Blue Hill Avenue
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Boston men were arrested in connection with a June mass shooting on Blue Hill Avenue, leaving one dead, four injured.
Cerron Wilcox-Buford and Laquan Burris face charges including assault to murder and assault with a dangerous weapon.
Wilcox-Buford and Burris are held on $1 million and $250,000 bail, respectively; both have pleaded not guilty.

title: Mass. is seeing ‘normal’ levels in cases of the explosive diarrhea parasite despite Midwestern outbreak, DPH says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts reported 18 cases of cyclosporiasis from May 1 to July 1, aligning with typical seasonal patterns.
The Midwestern outbreak of cyclospora, affecting over 400 people, hasn't impacted Massachusetts, according to state officials.
Cyclospora transmitted via contaminated food/water, with symptoms like diarrhea and nausea; previous outbreaks linked to fresh produce.

title: ‘Being at sea does change a person’: Tall Ships inspire Boston’s young sailors
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Sail Boston 250 brings tall ships and thousands of visitors, posing logistical challenges for local sailing programs.
Courageous Sailing Center used the Mystic River to continue youth programs during the event's busy period.
Community Boating, Inc. engaged festival-goers with educational tents and regular programs to promote sailing interest.

title: Massachusetts issues statewide air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts has issued a statewide air quality alert due to smoke from Canadian wildfires affecting air quality.
The alert is in effect through 12 a.m. Thursday, with air quality labeled 'unhealthy for sensitive groups.'
Residents, especially sensitive groups, are advised to limit prolonged outdoor activities and monitor symptoms like shortness of breath.

title: Look inside Oosterschelde, the historic Dutch tall ship at Sail Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Oosterschelde, a 164-foot Dutch tall ship from 1918, is open for public tours at Sail Boston 2026.
The ship will remain in Massachusetts, moving to Quincy for tours from July 16-19 before sailing to other countries.
Rowes Wharf Sea Grille offers special Dutch martinis and snacks in celebration of the Oosterschelde's visit.
